allowed_claim_text: We have a sealed live solution packet for the TDP-43 LCD mechanism-language problem: a source-separated sparse-aromatic/prion-like ensemble and perturbation model for residues 274-414, supported by post-seal holdout evidence. This is not a universal protein-folding solved claim.

context_shift_validation.predicted_shift: increases CTD self-association and condensate formation

context_shift_validation.context: temperature, salt, crowding, or hexanediol-like perturbation
context_shift_validation.predicted_shift: shifts reversible liquid condensates versus aggregates

context_shift_validation.context: RNA/DNA or multidomain TDP-43 context present
context_shift_validation.predicted_shift: changes viscoelasticity and competes with CTD self-association

context_shift_validation.context: CTD phosphorylation or phosphomimetic stress-pathology state
context_shift_validation.predicted_shift: shifts aggregation/condensate readouts and pathology-associated material state
perturbs helix/aromatic/prion-like grammar and can bias solid-like maturation

controls.reason: Prediction must be sealed before holdout validation.
Holdout files must be unavailable to prediction function.
PDB coordinates before sealing are blocked.
AlphaFold/ESMFold coordinates before sealing are blocked.
Internal runtime reports as biological evidence are blocked.
Generic IDP annotation alone cannot create a full solution packet.
FUS-LC grammar cannot be transferred as TDP-43 solution evidence.
Removing alpha-helical segment evidence weakens support.
Removing nucleic-acid context weakens context support.
Swapped non-TDP IDP evidence cannot validate TDP-43-specific predictions.
Failed predictions remain failed and are not repaired after holdout.
If fewer than four independent holdout classes exist, status is partial.
If all predictions are generic IDP claims, status fails.

falsification_criteria: TDP-43 LCD residues 274-414 reproducibly adopt one compact single native fold under ordinary conditions
W/F/Y aromatic sticker mutations do not alter phase behavior or self-association
321-343 helix-prone segment perturbation has no effect on LLPS or ensemble signatures
nucleic-acid or multidomain context does not shift TDP-43 condensate material properties when binding/context is present
ALS-linked CTD mutations do not perturb LLPS or aggregation readouts in any reproducible direction
FUS-LC dense tyrosine-ladder grammar validates TDP-43 LCD predictions without TDP-specific evidence
independent holdouts contradict more than two explicit perturbation predictions
forbidden_claims: we solved the universal protein-folding problem
TDP-43 LCD has one solved atomic native structure
FUS-LC grammar automatically solves all IDP LCDs
coordinates were predicted de novo for all TDP-43 states

holdout_sources.evidence_statement: TDP-43 CTD/LCD is annotated as disordered and low-complexity rather than a compact native domain.
holdout_sources.source_url_or_citation: DisProt DP01108/Q13148 disorder annotations and UniProt non-coordinate features

holdout_sources.evidence_statement: TDP-43 CTD LLPS is mediated by only a few aromatic residues.
holdout_sources.source_url_or_citation: PubMed 29511089, Li et al. J Biol Chem 2018
supports_context_switches: temperature, salt, crowding, or hexanediol-like perturbation

holdout_sources.evidence_statement: ALS mutations disrupt phase separation mediated by an alpha-helical segment in the low-complexity CTD.
The prion-like domain self-assembles, binds nucleic acid, and can undergo disorder-to-order transitions.
holdout_sources.source_url_or_citation: PubMed 26735904, Lim et al. PLoS Biol 2016
supports_context_switches: RNA/DNA or multidomain TDP-43 context present

holdout_sources.evidence_statement: Physical-force evidence supports CTD self-association and phase-separation modulation.
holdout_sources.source_url_or_citation: PubMed 28988034, Li et al. BBA Proteins Proteom 2018
supports_context_switches: temperature, salt, crowding, or hexanediol-like perturbation

holdout_sources.evidence_statement: PTM/pathology context supports phosphorylation as a material-state and aggregation readout shift, not as a native-fold solution.
holdout_sources.source_url_or_citation: UniProt Q13148 PTM/pathology context plus TDP-43 proteinopathy literature
supports_context_switches: CTD phosphorylation or phosphomimetic stress-pathology state

holdout_sources.evidence_statement: RNA and multidomain context modulate TDP-43 condensate interactions and viscoelasticity.
supports_context_switches: RNA/DNA or multidomain TDP-43 context present

low_resolution_ensemble_prediction.ensemble_type: glycine-rich prion-like IDP ensemble with sparse-sticker LLPS and disorder-to-order maturation
low_resolution_ensemble_prediction.fibril_or_solid_boundary: state/context-dependent CTD ordering, not a whole-domain native fold
low_resolution_ensemble_prediction.state_model: soluble CTD disorder <-> reversible LLPS condensate <-> mutation/stress shifted amyloid or solid-like branch, modulated by RNA/DNA and phosphorylation/pathology state

next_action: external review and direct TDP-43 LCD perturbation experiments before strengthening beyond live solution packet wording

next_decision.next_action: external review and direct TDP-43 LCD perturbation experiments before strengthening beyond live solution packet wording

perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: weakens LLPS/self-association more than a generic composition-preserving shuffle

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: disrupt W334-centered and F-rich sticker neighborhoods
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: reduces condensate formation and changes local NMR/ensemble signatures

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: mutate or break the 321-343 helix-prone CTD segment
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: disrupts TDP-43-specific phase separation without requiring a FUS-like tyrosine ladder
collapses the TDP-43 LCD grammar and blocks the proper LLPS-to-aggregation state model
shifts phase behavior, aggregation, or interaction readouts in mutation-specific directions

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: increase phosphorylation or phosphomimetic charge in the CTD/LCD
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: alters condensate/aggregate/pathology readouts rather than creating one folded state

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: add RNA/DNA or restore multidomain nucleic-acid-binding context
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: rebalance CTD self-association, viscoelasticity, and aggregation tendency

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: isolate C-terminal fragments or extend stress exposure
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: increases amyloid/solid-like maturation relative to the soluble disordered CTD ensemble
must be rejected; the LCD is an ensemble/phase/prion-like transition problem

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: transfer FUS-LC dense tyrosine-ladder grammar onto TDP-43 LCD
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: must fail as an overfit transfer because TDP-43 uses sparse aromatics plus helix/prion-like CTD grammar

perturbation_predictions.perturbation: alter methionine-rich mid-CTD and C-terminal tail clusters
perturbation_predictions.predicted_effect: shifts material-state balance and partner/context sensitivity

plain_english_interpretation: V45 produced a sealed, source-separated TDP-43 LCD live solution packet that is not just FUS-LC replay: it uses sparse aromatics, helix-prone CTD grammar, glycine-rich prion-like disorder, mutation modulation, nucleic-acid context, and amyloid/solid maturation as distinct operator language.

predicted_state_grammar.grammar: heterogeneous glycine-rich prion-like ensemble without one native fold
few aromatic stickers and helix-prone CTD contacts nucleate condensates
disorder-to-order conversion emerges in selected CTD windows
ALS-linked CTD substitutions alter LLPS and aggregation balance
nucleic acid and folded-domain context rebalances CTD self-association

proposed_experimental_tests: mutate W/F/Y stickers, especially W334 and nearby F-rich neighborhoods, and measure turbidity, microscopy, FRAP, and NMR shifts
break the 321-343 helix-prone CTD segment and compare phase behavior to wild type
compare ALS-linked CTD mutants across LLPS, aggregation, and nucleic-acid-binding contexts
test phosphomimetic and phospho-null variants in CTD condensate and aggregate assays
add RNA/DNA or restore multidomain context and quantify viscosity, elasticity, recruitment, and self-association shifts
compare full CTD 274-414 to C-terminal fragments under aging/stress for amyloid or solid-like maturation
